Free Text: There is a pond to the north of the station supporting a small population of Great Crested Newts which are legally protected and medium sized population of smooth newts and they have also been regularly encountered in the ballast on tracks.  Ensure that an Ecologist is consulted before planning works.  Some works may be undertaken in the attendance of an ecologist, but more high impact work may require a licence from Natural England before works can start.  Only licenced individuals can handle Great Crested Newts and it is an offence to handle or intentionally harm them.  What3words crunch.pencil.summer

ELR Name: CHURCH FENTON NORTH JN MICKLEFIELD JN
Free Text:
Limestone bedrock (Cadeby Formation) is known to form natural cavities.  These can result in sinkholes either from dissolution close to surface or from propagation collapse.  This may cause track performance issues, and possibly, track collapse.
Ground investigation and site walkovers have confirmed the presence of magnesium rich limestone.  Small cavities (less than 200mm) have been observed in some rock exposures.

Free Text: Recorded and unrecorded workings in coal, ironstone and sand in historical mines between Leeds and Micklefield pose risk to lineside assets..  Workings range between 6m bgl to 30m bgl (for shallow) as well as deeper workings down to at least 300m.  Shallower seams have typically been exploited by open cast means.   The workings are largely unrecorded, often found as part of investigations or conjectured due to site conditions and mine entries.  Risk is potential collapse of the surface through crown hole propagation, causing collapse or instability for foundations of lineside assets.

ELR Name: AREA BDY MICKLEFIELD TO LEEDS CITY STATION (4TH PART OF HULL - LEEDS)
Free Text: Recorded and unrecorded workings in coal, ironstone and sand in historical mines between Leeds and Micklefield pose risk to the track.  Workings range between 6m bgl to 30m bgl (for shallow) as well as deeper workings down to at least 300m.  Shallower seams have typically been exploited by open cast means.   The workings are largely unrecorded, often found as part of investigations or conjectured due to site conditions and mine entries.  Risk to Track is potential collapse of the surface through crown hole propagation, causing collapse or instability in the track.

Free Text:
This Hazard is applicable to 3 separate ELR"s, HUL4, DOL2 and TJC3. it also covers multiple TrackID"s.~
5km long 25kV interconnector cable between Armley TSL and Neville Hill TSC <AZ/NEHA>. ~
Cable runs in dedicated cable route along the Up side from Neville Hill through Richmond Hill cutting and Marsh Lane viaduct. The cable is installed along the back of Platform 16 /17 through Leeds station and then crosses track under the Leeds/Liverpool canal bridge. The cable then runs along the A road / Up Harrogate to Armley TSL.

Free Text: Access Point. Station. Leeds City centre.

ELR Name: AREA BDY MICKLEFIELD TO LEEDS CITY STATION (4TH PART OF HULL - LEEDS)
Free Text: Yorkshire Electricity 11kV cable runs underground down standford street to HV substation. Railtrack HV Cable O/head 4 underground

ELR Name: AREA BDY MICKLEFIELD TO LEEDS CITY STATION (4TH PART OF HULL - LEEDS)
Free Text: Asbestos ceiling and gutters.

ELR Name: AREA BDY MICKLEFIELD TO LEEDS CITY STATION (4TH PART OF HULL - LEEDS)
Free Text: Limit of 3 tonnes gross vehicle and plant weight limit applicable to all concourse and platform areas. Requests for dispensation up to a higher limit must be accompanied by a structural assessment and submitted to the Senior Asset Manager for consideration.

ELR Name: AREA BDY MICKLEFIELD TO LEEDS CITY STATION (4TH PART OF HULL - LEEDS)
Free Text: Limit of 3 tonnes gross vehicle and plant weight limit applicable to the Short stay car park area. Requests for dispensation up to a higher limit must be accompanied by a structural assessment and submitted to the Structures Manager for consideration.

ELR Name: AREA BDY MICKLEFIELD TO LEEDS CITY STATION (4TH PART OF HULL - LEEDS)
Free Text: North side of Bridge on New Station Street. Limit of 7.5 tonnes gross vehicle and plant weight limit applicable for the access on the north side of the bridge on New Station Street. Requests for dispensation up to a higher limit must be accompanied by a structural assessment and submitted to the Structures Manager for consideration.

ELR Name: AREA BDY MICKLEFIELD TO LEEDS CITY STATION (4TH PART OF HULL - LEEDS)
Free Text: An authorised walking route exists off the end of platform 17. This gives access to the Compressor House and also forms part of the station evacuation plan.

ELR Name: AREA BDY MICKLEFIELD TO LEEDS CITY STATION (4TH PART OF HULL - LEEDS)
Free Text: All tracks have overhead 25,000-volt AC electric traction supply cables. All works undertaken on the station must be carried out in accordance with the Network Rail instructions concerning personal safety on electrified lines

ELR Name: AREA BDY MICKLEFIELD TO LEEDS CITY STATION (4TH PART OF HULL - LEEDS)
Free Text: Requirement to disturb the surface within the station's controlled area, the Buried Services Procedure shall be followed at all times. Work will not be allowed to commence until such time that a Permit to Dig/Penetrate form has been duly signed by the Contract Administrator. Numerous low and high voltage cables are located underground throughout the station. Each contractor will be responsible for the scanning of the work site in order to locate the exact location of the underground power cables.

ELR Name: AREA BDY MICKLEFIELD TO LEEDS CITY STATION (4TH PART OF HULL - LEEDS)
Free Text: In addition to the above cabling, Leeds City Station contains a large quantity of operational equipment including Signalling and Telecommunications <S&T> cables and train dispatch equipment. Any contractor proposing to undertake any demolition, excavation or works that could disturb any cables should refer to the National Hazard Directory. Additional information on S&T equipment can be obtained from the Route Signalling Engineer.

ELR Name: AREA BDY MICKLEFIELD TO LEEDS CITY STATION (4TH PART OF HULL - LEEDS)
Free Text: A OHLE 25kv cable has been installed in an aco channel along the rear of platform 16/17.

ELR Name: AREA BDY MICKLEFIELD TO LEEDS CITY STATION (4TH PART OF HULL - LEEDS)
Free Text: There are numerous drainage runs located around the station which can be obtain on limited drawings from the Senior Asset Manager and the Facility Delivery Manager. These locations has been identified; Rail Gourmet on platform 8, Platforms 8A-8B-8C, South Concourse, North Concourse, Multi-storey & Riverside Car Parks, New Station Street road gullies, New Station Street Rail replacement pick up point and Dark arches.

ELR Name: AREA BDY MICKLEFIELD TO LEEDS CITY STATION (4TH PART OF HULL - LEEDS)
Free Text: Incoming water supplies to the station are located in the Dark Arches: Number 1 supply in Sandford Street arch on a underground meter. Number 2 supply in Dark Neville Street on a underground meter. Limited records of water supply pipelines are held by the Senior Asset Manager and the Facility Delivery Manager, though these will be present to most areas of the Station including platforms for the train watering system.

ELR Name: AREA BDY MICKLEFIELD TO LEEDS CITY STATION (4TH PART OF HULL - LEEDS)
Free Text: Incoming supplies to the station are located in: Neville Street via the former Time Office and in Sandford Street arch adjacent to the Queens Hotel entrance gate. Limited records of gas supply pipelines are held by the Senior Asset Manager and the Facility Delivery Manager. All mains gas supply pipes up to and including the meter are the responsibility of National Grid. In case of emergency contact 0800 111 999. Natural gas is non-toxic, but large concentrations could cause suffocation. It is highly flammable and leakage from a pipeline could result in an explosive mixture with air. Noise of escaping gas could be sufficient to cause damage to hearing and ear protection must be worn. All persons must be kept 100 metres from the damaged pipeline.

ELR Name: AREA BDY MICKLEFIELD TO LEEDS CITY STATION (4TH PART OF HULL - LEEDS)
Free Text: Asbestos hazards please see ARMS for specific details

ELR Name: AREA BDY MICKLEFIELD TO LEEDS CITY STATION (4TH PART OF HULL - LEEDS)
Free Text: Within the Arches and in the voids beneath the platforms there is the likelihood of the presence of vermin. This is particularly the case in those arches which are currently unused and contain large amounts of rubbish, thus providing a potential breeding ground. Contractors should be aware that poison has been put down at various locations.

ELR Name: AREA BDY MICKLEFIELD TO LEEDS CITY STATION (4TH PART OF HULL - LEEDS)
Free Text: These rooms are both placed on top of roofs. One is on the Adm. Block roof and the other boiler room is on top of the ticket office roof.

ELR Name: AREA BDY MICKLEFIELD TO LEEDS CITY STATION (4TH PART OF HULL - LEEDS)
Free Text: Dark Arches Complex - HV switch rooms. These rooms contain HV electrical equipment and have restricted access to persons with relevant safety training. A key will be issued by station management upon completion of a permit and production of proof of ID. This area is managed by E&P.

ELR Name: AREA BDY MICKLEFIELD TO LEEDS CITY STATION (4TH PART OF HULL - LEEDS)
Free Text: Service yard - HV switch room. This room contain HV electrical equipment and have restricted access to persons with relevant safety training. A key will be issued by station management upon completion of a permit and production of proof of ID. This area is managed by E&P.

ELR Name: AREA BDY MICKLEFIELD TO LEEDS CITY STATION (4TH PART OF HULL - LEEDS)
Free Text: West end Substation - HV switch room. High Voltage cables feeding this area will need to be traced and located prior to any contractor working in this area. The High Voltage cables supplying this location were installed as a ring and run both underground and overhead from the Main Sandford Street substation following the length of the Cross arch into Dark Neville Street. This room contain HV electrical equipment and have restricted access to persons with relevant safety training. A key will be issued by station management upon completion of a permit and production of proof of ID. This area is managed by E&P.

ELR Name: AREA BDY MICKLEFIELD TO LEEDS CITY STATION (4TH PART OF HULL - LEEDS)
Free Text: Each platform has a Low Voltage Switch Room. These rooms have Limited Access and extensive electrical equipment resulting in electrocution hazards. There is also cabling at height, which could lead to hazards from working at height and falling objects.

Free Text: West end compressor house of platform 17. Contained 3 compressors, air receivers and associated pipework.

WARNING: THE ENTRANCE TO THIS ACCESS POINT IS MONITORED BY CCTV 24/7 AND THE GATES AT THE TOP OF THE LANE AND AT THE RAILWAY ACCESS MUST BE CLOSED AND LOCKED IMMEDIATELY AFTER USE. Free Text:

5km long 25kV interconnector cable between Armley TSL and Neville Hill TSC <AZ/NEHA>. ~

The cable then runs along the A road / Up Harrogate to Armley TSL.

ELR Name: CHESTERFIELD (TAPTON JCN) - COLNE {MASBORO' C.O.M. - COLNE}(PART CLOSED)
Free Text: Circa 310no. Platypus steel anchors have been driven below both 1100 Up Main/Fast & 2100 Down Main Fast to support the new sheet pile retaining wall along the river Aire. These anchors are circa 11m long on average but this varies, most should be shorter than this, but there maybe instances of longer anchors. These anchors should be circa 5m below the railhead. However, due to an incident during the summer of 2024, one anchor deflected  and surfaced next to the railhead. The positioning of all the other anchors due to deflection may not be 5 m underground and could be a lot closer to the surface. However, there is no way following extensive research to know the exact depth of these anchors, so when undertaking track renewal or any excavations between the sheet piles and track there could be hidden anchors.  Around 2000, Platypus steel anchors were installed beneath both the 1100 Up Main/Fast and 2100 Down Main Fast tracks to support the new sheet pile retaining wall along the River Aire. These anchors typically average 11 meters in length, though this can vary, with most being shorter, though some may be longer. The anchors are generally expected to be positioned about 5 meters below the railhead. However, due to an incident in the summer of 2024, one anchor was deflected and surfaced near the railhead. As a result, the depth of the other anchors may also have shifted, meaning they may not be 5 meters deep and could be closer to the surface. Extensive research has been conducted, but there is no definitive way to determine the exact depth of each anchor. Therefore, when performing track renewal or any excavation work between the sheet piles and the tracks, there may be hidden anchors present.

Free Text: From Broughton Road <A6069>, Skipton, turn off Carleton New Road, crossing over the railway bridge <TJC3-90> and follow for approx 400m . From Carleton New Road turn into Engine Shed Lane and follow this road for approx 700m until reaching the turning for The Sidings. Follow The Sidings until the end of the public road and the pedestrian access gate is located immediately on the left hand side . Free Text: Unprotected watermain -25mm Dia. MDPE Pipe- crosses beneath both the UP & DOWN Lines at the 225 Milepost at Gargrave station. The pipe emerges from the Up platform wall face at approx 200mm below sleeper bottom level. The pipe then crosses beneath both the Up & Down mains at the same level and into the Down platform wall face. Water supply is to Station House behind the Down platform. The pipe is classed as "Unadopted" by Yorkshire Water, they are not currently responsible for damage repairs.
